seo_title: OmniToM Benchmark Evaluates LLMs' Theory of Mind Capabilities
meta_description: A new benchmark, OmniToM, assesses large language models' ability to model belief structures and track actors' mental states, revealing limitations in current…
body_html:
OmniToM, a new benchmark, directly evaluates large language models' ability to model belief structures and track actors' mental states.

  • Belief Extraction Stage:

In the OmniToM benchmark, models are first evaluated in a Belief Extraction stage, where they extract relevant beliefs from a story's social dynamics.

Belief Propositions Number of Propositions Proposition Type Context
Knowledge, Intentions, Emotions, False Beliefs 22,343 Belief Propositions Common Format

  • Belief Labeling Stage:

In the second stage, models assign each extracted belief a seven-dimensional schema label covering recursive order, truth status, knowledge access, explicitness, content type, mental source, and context.

  • Recursive Order
  • Truth Status
  • Knowledge Access
  • Explicitness
  • Content Type
  • Mental Source
  • Context

  • Results and Limitations:

The OmniToM benchmark reveals an actor-specific belief-tracking bottleneck in current LLMs, highlighting limitations in their ability to transform narrative facts into actors' beliefs and shared mental states.

  • Knowledge-Access Bottleneck
  • Representational Decisions

  • Future Directions:

The study suggests that explicit belief state representations can enhance task performance and accuracy of Theory of Mind inferences for LLM-based agents.

  • Explicit Belief State Representations
  • Task Performance Enhancement

Read time: 10 minutes

source.title: OmniToM: Benchmarking Theory of Mind in LLMs via Explicit Belief Modeling
source.summary: Researchers have introduced a new benchmark called OmniToM to evaluate Theory of Mind (ToM) in large language models (LLMs). The benchmark requires explicit modeling of belief structures for all relevant actors within a narrative, allowing for the analysis of knowledge, intentions, emotions, and false beliefs. In a zero-shot evaluation across diverse models, OmniToM reveals that current LLMs struggle with transforming narrative facts into actors' beliefs and shared mental states, particularly in terms of knowledge-access and representational decisions.
